mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 65% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during labor market deteriorations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that drives corporate revenue acceleration and sustains positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing immediate negative equity repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during late-cycle phases do not predict negative 5-day forward returns, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth exceeds 3.0 percent, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation trigger events, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) or positive payroll surprises yield negative equity returns in more than half of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that rapidly erodes corporate profit forecasts and triggers immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between GDP and retail sales declines fails to predict negative 5-day returns, or the joint signal produces positive returns in over 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and improving supply chain efficiency, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration during recovery phases does not yield positive 20-day forward returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) in recovery regimes.
